text.p: parts of Adelaide Hills, Adelaide, Onkaparinga and Playford council areas.
The State Emergency Service advises that people should:
* Don't drive, ride or walk through flood water.
* Keep clear of creeks and storm drains.
* Stay indoors, away from windows, while storms are nearby.

warning-info.text: The next warning is due to be issued by 8:40 pm.

text.p: Check https://www.bom.gov.au/weather-and-climate/warnings-and-alerts. Warnings are also available through TV and Radio broadcasts or call 1300 659 210. The Bureau and State Emergency Service would appreciate warnings being broadcast regularly.
A more general severe thunderstorm warning is also current for the Adelaide Metropolitan and parts of the Mount Lofty Ranges, North West Pastoral, Flinders and North East Pastoral districts.

The Bureau of Meteorology warns that, at 7:40 pm, a severe thunderstorm likely to produce heavy rainfall that may lead to flash flooding was detected near Glenelg and Henley Beach. This thunderstorm is moving towards the east. It is forecast to affect Adelaide City, Blackwood and Woodville by 8:10 pm and Tea Tree Gully, Modbury and Athelstone by 8:40 pm.

hazard.text: Onkaparinga, Adelaide Hills, Adelaide and Playford

text.p: The Bureau of Meteorology warns that, at 7:40 pm, a severe thunderstorm likely to produce heavy rainfall that may lead to flash flooding was detected near Glenelg and Henley Beach. This thunderstorm is moving towards the east. It is forecast to affect Adelaide City, Blackwood and Woodville by 8:10 pm and Tea Tree Gully, Modbury and Athelstone by 8:40 pm.
